label: Elbow tendinitis without rupture or neurovascular red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated elbow tendinitis.

No tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, distal biceps/triceps concern, elbow instability, ulnar nerve symptoms, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Tendon rupture, distal biceps or triceps concern, fracture or dislocation concern, elbow instability, ulnar nerve symptoms, open wound, infection concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, inability to use the limb, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
must_not_miss: Distal biceps or triceps tendon rupture.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, therapy, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing, overuse-related tendon pain, activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and separation from rupture, infection, trauma, or neurovascular compromise. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated elbow tendinitis with rupture, fracture, instability, ulnar nerve, infection, and neurovascular red flags absent.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing, overuse-related tendon pain, activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and separation from rupture, infection, trauma, or neurovascular compromise. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated elbow tendinitis with rupture, fracture, instability, ulnar nerve, infection, and neurovascular red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of uncomplicated elbow tendinitis
no infection or ulnar nerve symptoms
